name: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
description: EU regulation classifying AI systems by risk level. Full enforcement: August 2, 2026.
verification_note: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 is in force; the tier/category content below (Annex III sectoral categories, Article 5 prohibited practices, Article 50 transparency) is unaffected by the timeline change described here -- only the high-risk compliance deadline shifted. Article 5 prohibited-practice obligations (effective 2025-02-02) and GPAI obligations (effective 2025-08-02) are unaffected. RESOLVED 2026-07-10 (Week 3 parallel cleanup): the Digital Omnibus deferral is now formally adopted, not provisional -- the European Parliament endorsed the text 2026-06-16 and the Council of the EU gave final green light 2026-06-29; the act enters into force three days after Official Journal publication (expected shortly after 2026-06-29). Confirmed application dates: 2027-12-02 for stand-alone Annex III high-risk systems (this dataset's effective_from, above) and 2028-08-02 for high-risk AI embedded in products under Annex I. The package also adds a new Article 5 prohibition on generating non-consensual intimate imagery and CSAM, compliance required by 2026-12-02 -- NEEDS_OWNER_REVIEW: this new prohibited-practice ground is not yet reflected in the 'prohibited' tier's criteria/examples below and should be added as a distinct content update, not folded into this verification pass. Verified via web search 2026-07-09 (Week 2) and 2026-07-10 (Week 3): Council of the EU press release, 'Artificial Intelligence: Council gives final green light to simplify and streamline rules' (2026-06-29) (https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2026/06/29/artificial-intelligence-council-gives-final-green-light-to-simplify-and-streamline-rules/); Sidley, 'EU Lawmakers Reach Provisional Agreement to Delay Key EU AI Act Obligations' (2026-06-22) (https://datamatters.sidley.com/2026/06/22/eu-lawmakers-reach-provisional-agreement-to-delay-key-eu-ai-act-obligations/); Gibson Dunn, 'EU AI Act Omnibus Agreement' (https://www.gibsondunn.com/eu-ai-act-omnibus-agreement-postponed-high-risk-deadlines-and-other-key-changes/); Morgan Lewis (2026-06) (https://www.morganlewis.com/pubs/2026/06/eu-approves-delays-and-other-amendments-to-certain-eu-ai-act-obligations-what-businesses-should-know); EU AI Act Service Desk implementation timeline (https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/timeline/timeline-implementation-eu-ai-act).

criteria.summary: AI systems posing unacceptable risk. Banned in the EU.
examples: Real-time biometric identification in public spaces (with narrow exceptions)
Exploitation of vulnerabilities of specific groups
Biometric categorization based on sensitive characteristics
Untargeted scraping of facial images for facial recognition databases

criteria.summary: AI systems used in specific high-impact sectors. Subject to extensive compliance requirements.

annex_iii_categories.1_biometrics: Biometric identification, categorization, and emotion recognition
annex_iii_categories.2_critical_infrastructure: Critical infrastructure management (traffic, water, gas, electricity)
annex_iii_categories.3_education: Education and vocational training (admission, evaluation, assessment)
annex_iii_categories.4_employment: Employment, worker management, access to self-employment (recruitment, promotion, termination decisions)
annex_iii_categories.5_essential_services: Access to essential services: (a) public benefits eligibility, (b) credit scoring or creditworthiness evaluation of natural persons, (c) risk assessment and pricing of life and health insurance, (d) emergency response services
annex_iii_categories.8_justice_democracy: Administration of justice and democratic processes

criteria.summary: AI systems with specific transparency obligations. Users must be informed they are interacting with AI.
All other AI systems. Voluntary codes of conduct only.
